response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a highly experienced individual contributor (~20 years) with genuine developer tooling credentials — CI/CD infrastructure, test automation frameworks, and MCP tooling — but this is a Level II people-management role and the resume contains zero evidence of managing direct reports, conducting 1:1s, running performance cycles, or leading hiring. Staff titles across his career reflect IC seniority, not management scope. The job's minimum threshold of 2 years people management is unmet, and there is no organizational leadership experience to suggest a credible path to the responsibilities described. His technical profile is strong but fundamentally misaligned with the role's core accountability.
strengths: ~20 years of software development breadth with sustained developer tooling work across CI/CD infrastructure, test runners (Trial, Fluent-Asserts), and MCP code generators at Stripe
Some mobile platform exposure via iOS and Apple TV development at WatchUp and mobile device orchestration tooling at Altom, partially addressing the preferred mobile-ecosystem requirement
Active knowledge-sharing history through workshops, open-source libraries, and conference talks, suggesting ability to level up engineers around him
concerns: No people management experience anywhere in the resume — no direct reports, no performance ownership, no hiring, no org-level accountability at any employer
Entire career trajectory is IC-only; Staff titles at Altom and Stripe denote technical seniority, not leadership scope over people or teams

Developer tooling contributions are fragmented — personal open-source projects and side infrastructure work rather than sustained ownership of a productivity platform serving a large engineering organization

key_factors.factor: IC-only career with no management trajectory
Genuine developer tooling and test automation depth
